tokens: ...about your next assignments coming up here.
Dave, we want you to drop Jim at the LM and I'll talk to him in a minute.
And then you go on over park near ALSEP, headed west.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) Boy, there sure are a lot of neat rocks in the Dune.
Too bad we can't spend some more time.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, you might want to start the camera.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Think we can get through up here?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I think you should be able to.
Maybe a little to the right?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) And we're in the little boulder field.
And about a foot, at the biggest, down to about 6 inches.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, it looks like from a crater that hit on the rim of Dune.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're reading on a bearing of 350, range 3.3.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, and we're on our tracks.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Gee, it's nice to sit down, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You hop off and work like mad for 10 minutes and hop back on, sit down, and take a break.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I guess in a couple craters we remarked that we saw a boulder distribution that looked like it was linear, like it was a ray pattern.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: But we never did get a chance to really sample any of those.
As I recall, we saw one on the, what, south side of the Dune, on the way down.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We could probably save some time going back by not following the tracks, you know, because...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think we ought to head right straight ahead.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The only big crater over that way would probably be Earthlight.
(Pause) I think that's probably Earthlight that we see at 12 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: If we stay west of Earthlight, we ought to save a little distance.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, (but) let's get out of this little boulder field first.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, now we'll take a little left here, and keep...Oh well; we can look at Pluton.
We'll see Pluton all the way.
And the LM is silhouetted right against the base of Pluton so we can't miss that.
And just to the right of it is Schaber Hill which we'll be heading for tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, by the way, Joe, I guess we ought to tell you about what we saw at that last stop.
We gathered a few quick samples that were covered with dust, which we didn't look at very carefully, just so we could get ahold of them.

Then the very large boulder, which was probably about 6-feet (tall) sticking up out of the ground, with a very large 3- to 4-inch vesicles, was a very fine-grained, dark, black, basalt, with maybe, gee, I'd say 15-percent plage in it, wouldn't you, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And on the top, it had some smaller millimeter-size vesicles, and adjacent to it was another, lighter-gray, vesicular basalt, which was uniform in vesicularity, and which we didn't have time to sample, but the vesicles in that looked similar to that one rock that we got yesterday, Jim.
Remember that was in the bag alone.
Anyway, these vesicles were, gee, I'd say 4 millimeters to...some of them, were a centimeter all the way through it.
And the two rocks seemed to be in contact with each other.
Unfortunately, we didn't have time to sample the second one, but we did get a fairly good sample of the corner of the first one and the central part near one of the vesicles.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, you might stop the camera now.
It's probably run through the film load, and we'd like clicks (speed) and amps reading please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, we're doing...Well, that can wait.
Okay, the camera is empty, Joe, (grunting slightly, probably as he leans forward to shut the camera off) so we got some coverage there.
(Pause) And we're going at about 10 clicks; amps reads about 10.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That might be Earthlight up ahead, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I guess we'd better go east of it, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We might end up on our tracks.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think I'll make it up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Cross-Sun (driving) is pretty good, you know?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I guess we looked over at Earthlight, didn't we?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Commented on the southern rim of it (at
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, we're in good shape now!
See the old LM sitting out there?
Start making out detail on it.
I think we're closer than that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: See how it checks out when we get there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, I've got some words for your next task when you arrive at the LM, if you're ready (to listen).
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Dave, we want you to stop at the LM and you'll have to off-load your collection bags and get configured for the next part of the EVA.
Jim, we want you to run a mal(function) procedure on your camera.

If you can't get that mag to work, put on mag Kilo Kilo and then your first job will be the LM site pans and then photographs of the descent engine and photographs of the solar-wind-composition experiment - the window shade.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'll talk you through the reconfiguration of the equipment when you're ready for it.
I think we have a good handle on it down here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Joe, why don't you wait till we get to the Rover (meaning the LM)?
Because it'll just slip right through us right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: So, wait until we get there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll be standing by, and when it's time, I just talk you through it.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: As far as Dave's bag, Joe.
I forget the number on it, but I'll just take out the core tubes that we have not used, and then it will be ready to go in SRC 2.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Do you agree with that, as far as Dave's bag?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll think it over once again here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Dave) Think you can get through there, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm going to go around to the right.
There's a directional set of ejecta; isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's right straight out one side.
It would be a good place to take a radial sample.
That thing came in from...Let's see, we'll be...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, we're going north and the ejecta pattern is spread out due west about 20 meters across; and it must go out a good 150 meters or so.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And our bearing is 347 and our range 2.0 at that point.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughing) Some of those babies sneak up on you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: This time we ought to get the covers up in the cabin, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You know, the Bull Durham sacks.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You got to go around to the right.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Be advised that the ALSEP is picking up the rumble of the Rover rolling across the plains.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Joking) And according to our data, you're heading right for the LM.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Jim, I think...(Responding to Joe) Aah!
Yeah, you can give us a DF on the seismometer.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, but I still think we were straighter...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Jim) Hey, I think this is Index, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The one on our left here?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: In fact, I'm pretty sure it's Index.
It's got the nice side crater in the north.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I thought Index had a larger crater though on the north side.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, I don't know how large "large" is anymore.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Chuckle) I give up on distances and sizes.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We might as well just head on over those tracks, because we know we're straight...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Notice that crater at 12:30 to us now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It's fresh and has a very light albedo.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, we're heading 360; the bearing's 340; and the range 1.5.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we were pointing right at November at the time.
So, Index is over there on the right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughing) Making me seasick (because of the bumpy, rolling ride).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...do you expect traveling on the mare.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And it's a new outdoor record.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: But, you know, it sure doesn't kick up as much as I thought it would.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, even if you had rubber tires on here, I think you'd kick up the same amount of dust.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And you sure wouldn't climb that hill (referring to the climb to Station 6) like we did.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Notice that white-colored rock there that we just went...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're still going at 12 clicks; heading 340.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And I'm wondering if you caught sight of the small crater you saw outbound, which you described as having bedrock in the bottom?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I don't think we've come that far yet.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Are you contemplating a stop there?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I see it dead a(head)...(Stops to listen to Joe)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...in a Nav reading; an odometer reading.
And we're going to measure how far away that is from the LM.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You can use that Rover for everything.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Looks like November has a lot of blocks, too, but I can't see any (brief pause) bedrock.
I thought the crater that looked like it had bedrock was off to the east of our tracks.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I was thinking of a different one, Jim.
(Pause) November has a raised rim which is, I think, unique around here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Kind of a large rock to the north of November.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That looks like it's half buried.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Now, this fresh crater that we're coming up ahead.
I know we talked about it but I don't know whether there was any bedrock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, we'll see when we get there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Long Pause) Having a bite to eat.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I think this is the one we called.
There are frags on the side.
It's got a light albedo; it's relatively fresh.
I think this is the one, isn't it?
Well, we'll give it to them anyway.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, a comment about your equipment off load.
The off-loading should go exactly as if it were at the end of EVA-1 with regards to transferring core tubes, and so forth.
Only the collection bag numbers will be different.
And we'll try to talk you through that.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Dave) I just didn't see that crater that I...That wasn't the one, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, because it was to the east of us and I looked out and saw a layer of bedrock about a quarter of the way up on the wall.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I haven't been able to see it on the way back.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're almost back to the LM.
(Pause) The freshest one was the one that Dave just gave you the coordinates on.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Unless this might have been the one.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That wasn't the one, that's too large.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I wonder if that's the best position for thermal.
I'm looking at the LM antenna.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The radar antenna's pointing straight up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Guess it is, that's the white side.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, it looks like a thoroughfare.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; we'll take this fork here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave, as you know, the only thing we have to worry about, especially with regard to kicking dirt, is the solar wind composition experiment and the LR-Cubed, which is pretty far away.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And we'll mark when you stop.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, Jim, I'm going to drop you off right here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Run through what you want to do now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, the first thing I guess...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...is to off load the gear as if you were out at the ALSEP site, with regard to transferring cores, et cetera.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, I got to get off, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, standing by for your LRV readouts, if you're still there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'm reading (heading) 004, (bearing) 018; (distance driven) 12.5; (range) 00.2; (amp-hours) 91, 98; (battery temperatures) 92, 98, and motor temps are low.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, are you climbing off the Rover now?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Responding to Joe) Yeah, we're climbing off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me ask you a question now.
You want us to configure now like we would at the ALSEP site?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, basically, you just want to unload the collection bags that you're carrying.
We want to wind up with collection bag number 2 on the hand tool carrier and number 3 under Jim's seat.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...we want number 5 on the hand tool carrier.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: 2 and 5 on the hand tool carrier.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And 2 is under Jim's seat right now.
We want to trade that out for number 3 going under the seat.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'm wondering if we should take our tools off (the PLSSs).
We might as well take our tools off, right, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...and we're going to ask you to deploy the flag a little later, and you probably will need the hammer for that.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No, I won't need the hammer (because of the soft soil).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I can't get that mag to operate, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think I'm going to be forced to...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Put on mag Kilo Kilo on that camera, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I was wondering: is Dave going to need his camera out there?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, why don't you take mine.
Mine happens to have Kilo on it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't know where that goes, but I've got two on it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: See, this is EVA-3 bag here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yes, so I'll just put it here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...number 2 should be under that seat as well.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Joe, let's see you want 2 and 5 on the hand tool carrier, and the rest under the seat, is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave, we do want you to unload the tools.
Clip 'em them back on the Hand Tool Carrier.
We'll need them later at the ALSEP site, perhaps.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'd like to take this little cargo here, and take it right over to the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, that's the bag that goes in...(It's) bag 5.
And bag 5 goes in the SRC.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me just take out the unused core tubes.
Joe, speak up now if there is anything else you want to put in bag 5.
(No answer) I'm going to take it over to the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, there's no sense in putting bag 5 on the hand-tool carrier, Joe, because it's just about full.
Why don't we put it on the MESA or in the SRC, or something.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, when you take your camera off, just leave it on my (Rover) seat.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, the only problem is, if we're able to get the deep (core) samples using the drill stems, we'd like them in the SRC.
I guess we'll leave it up to you, your choice.
It may be better just to take bag 5 over there right now and forego that little nicety.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, Joe, you didn't...Just a minute, Jim.
Now, Joe, you didn't say anything about getting deep cores.
That's why...(To Jim) Here, let's take 3 and put it over there.
Let me take 2 back, because now that I know that they want to try and get the deep cores, we do need bag 2.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's the first time anybody said anything about that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, Dave, why not leave 5 there!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: If you get the cores, bring them back, and we'll put it in there.
I just won't load it in the SRC.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, Jim, I'm going to leave you my camera...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, just hand it to me now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yes, let me read the numbers on it first.
Of course, it's not on all the way, but it's reading 89.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, you're hung up on that strap.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Listen, why don't you take my tools off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think we only have two bags to go up (into the cabin) this time.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me check yours again, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I don't recall that I tidied yours.
Okay; when you get in, your right side is loose.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Put these core tube caps under the seat.
We got more core tube caps now than we know what to do with.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Listen, those rocks that are under the seat.
I'll put those in bag...Well, the bag that's under there, before you drive off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, there isn't any bag under there now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, here's a bag right here.
Right, or is that the sam(ple)...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You're going to use that one out there, right?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Where do you want bag 7, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think it's under the seat, and I think it's the bag for the next EVA.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, that's one for the next EVA.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I was going to get those rocks and put it in this bag.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: My arms are a little longer, maybe I can reach it, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't want to leave any rocks there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Is that the only one, or do we have another one?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll just hold up on the SRC closing until...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...you decide what you're going to do out there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: There's a couple of samples there we probably ought to put in here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Sounds good, and, Jim, while you're working there, once again...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...we want you to get your LM site pans...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...pictures of the descent engine and solar wind composition pictures, and then I'll be back at you with the next job.
And, Dave, standing by for when you're ready.
And, I'll talk about your task coming up here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Are you through with the Hand Tool Carrier, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to tidy up the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Joe, back at the...On the MESA, I have bags 3, 5, and 6.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Joe, I'm on the Rover and ready to go.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We want you to park east of the ALSEP heading toward the west, and as far east as is comfortable for you.
Once again with the dust problem in mind.
And we want you to clean the TV camera and LCRU before you leave the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, I'll just get it fairly close to the Central Station and avoid the dust.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just don't drive too far west.
Keep it east, if you could, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Incidentally, at the Rover (meaning the LM), our bearing was 018 and range 0.2.
That's pretty good for a trip like that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) And, Dave, as you climb off there and get the TV set and are ready, I'll talk to you about the next drilling job.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay, I'm going to park right here.
And if you get bored (watching the drilling), there's a big chunk of dark-gray breccia with white clasts right in front of the left wheel.
Have fun looking at that, maybe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...we know you're not too close to the heat flow experiment holes now.
We do want you to be particularly aware of the SIDE which has had its dust cover pulled off of it, too.
So it'll be particularly sensitive to dust.
And, when you are ready, once again, I've got words on how this drill should work.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, first you want me to dust off your TV.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Alright, you broke up, Joe, I...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Do you want your TV lens dusted off?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...dust off the TV and the LCRU, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: TV, I understand that, but do you want the TV lens, because we have a (cooling) mirror (on the top of the TV camera) we can dust off and we also have a lens we can dust off.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, sir, both the camera and the lens, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: It takes a couple different brushes.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Your eye is all cleaned off.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Dave, we're standing by for TV turn on.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Put the brush back in its proper place.
(Pause) And I'll try and point the antenna.
(Pause) Again, not a very good position.
transcript.speaker: Scott
